name: workflow-plugin-crm
description: Vendor-neutral CRM plugin for the workflow engine (Salesforce adapter)
author: GoCodeAlone

stepSchemas.description: Creates a new record in the CRM system for the given object type.

configFields.label: CRM Module
configFields.description: Name of the crm.provider module instance to use.

configFields.label: Object Type
configFields.description: CRM object type to create (e.g. Account, Contact, Lead).

configFields.label: Fields
configFields.description: Map of field names to values for the new record.

outputs.description: ID of the created record.
Whether the record was created successfully.
List of error messages if the creation failed.
Error message if the step encountered a fatal error.

stepSchemas.description: Retrieves a single CRM record by object type and record ID. On success the record's fields are returned as top-level output keys.

configFields.label: CRM Module
configFields.description: Name of the crm.provider module instance to use.

configFields.label: Object Type
configFields.description: CRM object type to retrieve (e.g. Account, Contact).

configFields.label: Record ID
configFields.description: ID of the CRM record to retrieve.

outputs.description: CRM record ID (provider-standard field present on every record).
CRM record name field (provider-standard field, present on most object types).
Error message if the step failed or the record was not found. Additional provider-specific record fields are returned as sibling output keys.

stepSchemas.description: Updates an existing CRM record with the provided field values.

configFields.label: CRM Module
configFields.description: Name of the crm.provider module instance to use.

configFields.label: Object Type
configFields.description: CRM object type of the record to update.

configFields.label: Record ID
configFields.description: ID of the CRM record to update.

configFields.label: Fields
configFields.description: Map of field names to updated values.

outputs.description: Whether the record was updated successfully.
Error message if the step failed.

stepSchemas.description: Creates or updates a CRM record matched by an external ID field.

configFields.label: CRM Module
configFields.description: Name of the crm.provider module instance to use.

configFields.label: Object Type
configFields.description: CRM object type to upsert.

configFields.label: External ID Field
configFields.description: API name of the external ID field used to match existing records.

configFields.label: External ID Value
configFields.description: Value of the external ID field used to match existing records.

configFields.label: Fields
configFields.description: Map of field names to values to set on the record.

outputs.description: ID of the created or updated record.
Whether the upsert was successful.
Error message if the step failed.

stepSchemas.description: Deletes a CRM record by object type and record ID.

configFields.label: CRM Module
configFields.description: Name of the crm.provider module instance to use.

configFields.label: Object Type
configFields.description: CRM object type of the record to delete.

configFields.label: Record ID
configFields.description: ID of the CRM record to delete.

outputs.description: Whether the record was deleted successfully.
Error message if the step failed.

stepSchemas.description: Executes a structured query (e.g. SOQL for Salesforce) against the CRM and returns matching records.

configFields.label: CRM Module
configFields.description: Name of the crm.provider module instance to use.

configFields.label: Query
configFields.description: Query string in the provider's query language (e.g. SOQL).

outputs.description: List of records returned by the query.
Total number of records matched by the query.
Whether all matching records have been returned.
Error message if the step failed.

stepSchemas.description: Executes a free-text search (e.g. SOSL for Salesforce) across CRM objects.

configFields.label: CRM Module
configFields.description: Name of the crm.provider module instance to use.

configFields.label: Search Query
configFields.description: Search string in the provider's search language (e.g. SOSL FIND clause).

outputs.description: List of records returned by the search.
Number of records returned.
Error message if the step failed.

stepSchemas.description: Performs a bulk CRM operation (insert, update, upsert, or delete) on a set of records.

configFields.label: CRM Module
configFields.description: Name of the crm.provider module instance to use.

configFields.label: Object Type
configFields.description: CRM object type to operate on.

configFields.label: Operation
configFields.description: Bulk operation type: insert, update, upsert, or delete.

configFields.label: Records
configFields.description: Array of record field maps to process.

outputs.description: Provider job ID for the bulk operation.
Current state of the bulk job.
Number of records successfully processed.
Number of records that failed to process.
Error message if the step failed.

stepSchemas.description: Returns metadata describing a CRM object type, including its fields and their properties. On success the provider's describe payload is returned as top-level output keys.

configFields.label: CRM Module
configFields.description: Name of the crm.provider module instance to use.

configFields.label: Object Type
configFields.description: CRM object type to describe (e.g. Account, Contact).

outputs.description: API name of the CRM object type.
List of field metadata objects describing each field on the object.
Error message if the step failed. Additional provider-specific describe keys are returned as sibling output keys.

stepSchemas.description: Retrieves current API usage limits and remaining quota from the CRM provider. On success the provider's limits payload is returned as top-level output keys.

configFields.label: CRM Module
configFields.description: Name of the crm.provider module instance to use.

outputs.description: Object with Max and Remaining counts for daily API request quota (Salesforce standard key; other providers may use different keys).
Error message if the step failed. Additional provider-specific limit keys are returned as sibling output keys.
